data.title: Movement (MOVE) - AMA with Bitget - 10 Mar 2025
data.description: The upcoming AMA with Bitget for Movement (MOVE) provides crucial information about its ecosystem following the Mainnet launch. Such events can drive investor excitement by revealing new opportunities and project developments. The inclusion of giveaways adds further appeal, encouraging community participation and possibly boosting MOVE's visibility. AMAs highlight project transparency and may attract new investors, fostering price support or increase. By detailing ecosystem benefits, Movement can clarify its utility, potentially influencing market sentiment positively. Watch for any new strategic announcements that could signal future price moves. source

data.title: Ethernity Chain (ERN) - Rebrand to EPIC - 13 Mar 2025
data.description: Ethernity Chain's rebrand to Epic Chain (EPIC) is a major change, backed by a 97.1% DAO community vote. This rebrand includes a token swap at a 1:1 ratio, ensuring holders maintain their value. Major exchanges like Binance supporting the transition indicates strong market confidence. Such a shift can lead to increased interest and possibly higher prices due to renewed investor focus. Investors should watch closely as this could lead to significant price movements as it represents a shift in project direction and renewed marketing efforts. source

data.title: Entangle (NGL) - Emergency Transfer Pause - 09 Mar 2025
data.description:
The emergency pause on NGL transfers aims to tackle security concerns related to unauthorized token minting. This quick action reassures holders about project resilience, yet introduces short-term uncertainty. Such incidents can trigger volatility as investors react to potential risks associated with their holdings. While the pause may protect the project’s integrity, it could also lead to temporary sell pressure if confidence wavers. The outcome of the investigation and subsequent token burn will be crucial for restoring trust in NGL. Investors should remain cautious and stay informed. source

Entangle
@Entanglefi

We are implementing an emergency pause on all NGL token transfers to prevent any further minting by the exploiter.

Our team is actively investigating the situation, and once resolved, we will proceed with burning the excess minted tokens.

This incident has not affected our…

Mar 09, 2025

data.title: China inflation data: February CPI comes in at -0.7% y/y (expected -0.5%)
data.description:
China inflation data for February 2025:

Consumer Price Index -0.7% y/y, falling back into negative for the first time since January 2024

  • expected -0.5%, prior +0.5%

Consumer Price Index -0.2% m/m

  • expected -0.1%, prior +0.7%

Producer Price Index -2.2% y/y

  • expected -2.1%, prior -2.3%

China's National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) blames the y/y drop back into deflation for the CPI on:

  • higher base in the same month last year due to the shifting Spring Festival (Lunar New Year) dates
  • holiday impacts - last year's rise in food and services prices created a high comparison base
    • the NBS says that if this were adjusted for, the CPI would have been +0.1%
  • volatile international commodity prices
  • weather impacts - favourable vegetable growing weather this February compared to poor weather (rains and snow, pushing up prices) in February 2024 (base effect)

On the PPI the NBS cite:

  • holidays and cold-weather halted many construction projects, weakening demand for building materials
  • ample coal supply during the Spring Festival holidays led to coal processing prices fallling

The blaming of cold-weather impacting PPI seems at odds with it being favourable for growing vegetables. I'm not a construction worker, nor a farmer, so maybe my pointing this out is not of much value.

If anyone wants more, let me know in the comments and I'll do more digging on Monday.

Background (I posted this on Friday, repeating here):

China’s CPI showed a general downtrend from August to December 2024, reflecting weak consumer demand, before rebounding in January 2025 due to seasonal spending.

Meanwhile, PPI remained firmly in deflationary territory, suggesting that industrial sector challenges persist despite government stimulus efforts.

The data indicates that while consumer inflation has fluctuated, factory prices remain under pressure, signaling ongoing concerns about China's economic recovery.

In more detail:

All the below monthly results are y/y Consumer Price Index (CPI):

  • August 2024: +0.5% – The highest rise since early 2024, signaling some inflationary pressure.
  • September 2024: +0.4% – Slight slowdown from August but still indicating moderate price growth.
  • October 2024: +0.3% – Continued deceleration, reflecting subdued consumer demand.
  • November 2024: +0.2% – The lowest increase since June, raising concerns about deflation risks.
  • December 2024: +0.1% – Further slowdown, indicating weak domestic demand.
  • January 2025: +0.5% – A notable rebound, largely due to increased consumer spending during Lunar New Year.

All the below monthly results are y/y Producer Price Index (PPI):

  • August 2024: -2.3% – Continued factory-gate price deflation, reflecting weak industrial demand.
  • September 2024: -2.5% – Further decline, signaling persistent deflationary pressure in the industrial sector.
  • October 2024: -2.9% – The sharpest drop in months, marking the 25th consecutive month of factory price declines.
  • November 2024: -2.5% – Slight improvement from October, but factory deflation remains significant.
  • December 2024: -2.3% – Deflationary trend continues, extending to 27 straight months of decline.
  • January 2025: -2.3% – No significant improvement, indicating ongoing challenges in the industrial sector.

MIDEAST - Factors to watch - March 9
data.description:
Here are some factors that may affect Middle East stock markets on Sunday. Reuters has not verified the press reports and does not vouch for their accuracy.

INTERNATIONAL/REGIONAL

* GLOBAL MARKETS- Stocks, yields edge higher; Powell says economy still in good place

* Oil up, but off highs as Trump warns new Russia sanctions possible

* PRECIOUS- Gold heads for weekly gain on safe-haven demand, slow US job growth

* MIDEAST STOCKS- UAE stocks decline over tariff uncertainty

* Trump administration ends Iraq's waiver to buy Iranian electricity

* Israel, Hamas signal readiness for next ceasefire talks as mediators push for progress

* France, Germany, Italy, Britain back Arab plan for Gaza reconstruction

SAUDI ARABIA

* Saudis welcome chance to host US-Ukraine talks in Jeddah next week

* Saudi Arabia welcomes hosting meeting between US And Ukraine next week, foreign ministry says

* Trump says he will go to Saudi Arabia to reach $1 trillion deal

* Ukraine's Zelenskiy says he is going to Saudi Arabia on Monday, will meet Saudi crown prince

* Saudi Arabia cuts oil prices to Asia for first time in three months

* Saudi Arabia lowers April Arab Light OSP for Asia [Saudi Arabia lowers April Arab Light OSP for Asia]

UNITED ARAB EMIRATES

* ADNOC eyes international listing of XRG, sources say

* E&S startup MGT Specialty secures A-minus AM Best rating

* UAE’s Adnoc said to seek deals for gas fields in major US push- Bloomberg News

* UAE will seek immediate dismissal of case by Sudan at ICJ

QATAR

* Israel to send delegation to Doha for ceasefire talks

EGYPT

* Hamas delegation arrives in Cairo to discuss Gaza ceasefire agreement, Egypt says

Fed not cutting rates 'at all' in 2025 may trigger a bear market — Analyst
data.description:
Network economist Timothy Peterson warns that if the US Federal Reserve holds off on rate cuts in 2025, it may cause a broader market downturn, potentially dragging Bitcoin back toward $70,000.

“What it needs is a trigger. I think that trigger may be as simple as the Fed not cutting rates at all this year,” Peterson said in a March 8 X post. Peterson’s comment comes just a day after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reiterated that he's in no hurry to adjust interest rates.

Fed rate cut delay may spark bear market

"We do not need to be in a hurry and are well-positioned to wait for greater clarity,” Powell said in a speech in New York on March 7.

Peterson, who is the author of the paper “Metcalfe’s Law as a Model for Bitcoin’s Value,” estimated how low the Nasdaq could fall to predict Bitcoin’s potential bottom in a bear market.

Using Peterson’s Nasdaq lowest price forward model, Peterson estimated that the bottom would take about seven months to form, with the Nasdaq dropping 17% over the period.

Applying a “1.9” times multiplier to that amount for Bitcoin’s decline, he estimated a 33% decline in Bitcoin, bringing it down to $57,000 from its current price at publication, $86,199, according to CoinMarketCap data.

However, he said Bitcoin likely won’t drop that far, expecting a bottom closer to the low $70,000 range based on historical trends from 2022.

“Traders and opportunists hover over Bitcoin like vultures,” he said, explaining that once the market expects Bitcoin to hit $57,000, “it won’t get there because there are always some investors who step in because the price is ‘low enough.”

Bitcoin's 2022 low didn’t drop as expected

“I remember in 2022 when everyone said the bottom would be $12k. It only went to $16k, 25% higher than expected,” he said before pointing out that the 25% increase from $57,000 is $71,000. 

The last time Bitcoin traded at the $71,000 price level was on Nov. 6, after Donald Trump won the US election, before rallying for a month and reaching $100,000 by Dec. 5.

However, in January 2025, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes echoed a similar price prediction.

“I am calling for a $70k to $75k correction in BTC a mini financial crisis, and a resumption of money printing that will send us to $250k by the end of the year,” Hayes said in a Jan. 27 X post.

In December 2024, crypto mining firm Blockware Solutions said Bitcoin’s “bear case” for 2025 would be $150,000, assuming the Federal Reserve reverses course on interest rate cuts.
